replies.msg:
請問這裡有無負責人，為甚麼這裡有這麼多限制級、成人影片， 
而沒人監管或移到成人版， 
本來我讓小朋友來貴壇，是嚴禁到成人區， 
以為就此沒問題。 
怎知道他們來到這裡，看到了那些限制級影片， 
變得心理開始有問題。 
請趕快妥善處理，否則，我向相關部門投訴。

學術真係好難行 ==> yes.... dunno what to do.... no private life

今日比大學reject 我土炮mphil application
兩個老細一年前傾掂數話收硬，我個project都做咗大半年
而家學校先嚟話唔收 好想死

replies.msg:
做嘅field冇咩eye catching 嘅application...老細好鐘意做完fundamental 野就是但搵個journal pub 完就算，點到即止明明可以行遠啲但佢唔願意，pub完就棄左個proj

好難pud到高IF journal，原本想mphil pub多啲，phd另謀出路，但所有野IF都太低都唔知點開口搵phd supervisor

replies.msg:
懶人包(求補充)(唔sure 有冇聽漏)
0. (好似有) leave WHO
1. 唔比部份中國學生入境
2. 關於中國公司上市
3. begin to elinmiate treaties (extradition, travel advisory, tech expot, trade, 特立獨稅區)
4. 制裁人

好似得咁多
求指正
不過好猛料

replies.msg:
想問多唔多人係bachelor冇讀到master用experience, gpa, gre 成績入phd/psyd? (Psychology field)
我見master嘅course（即便係外國都唔多，香港...打擾了）有啲學府直情冇psychology master讀，combine埋落phd到，近年仲有個psyd
（探下路諗下應該點行攞experience）

replies.msg: More than two decades ago on a rainy night in 1997, British soldiers lowered the Union flag and Chinese soldiers raised the Chinese flag in Hong Kong. The people of Hong Kong felt simultaneously proud of their Chinese heritage and their unique Hong Kong identity. The people of Hong Kong hoped that in the years and decades to come, China would increasingly come to resemble it’s most radiant and dynamic city. The rest of the world was electrified by a sense of optimism that Hong Kong was a glimpse into China’s future, not that Hong Kong would grow into a reflection of China’s past.

The rest of the world was electrified by a sense of optimism that Hong Kong was a glimpse into China’s future, not that Hong Kong would grow into a reflection of China’s past.

replies.msg:
有3個問題,想請教大家：

1.）讀完PhD 之後Pathway 係點？
PhD —> Postdoc/ Lecturer —> Assistant Professor？

2.）香港既U請人，係咪會用以下既既Priority?？
（定其實唔關事？）

a. 英美一流名校 (G5, Ivy League, UCLA...etc)
b. 香港3大 = 英美二流名校（NYU, Edinburgh...)
c. 英美普通學校 (QS 500 打後）
d. 香港非3大

3.） 終身教職 (Tenure) 難度係咪極高？

pinned.msg: More than two decades ago on a rainy night in 1997, British soldiers lowered the Union flag and Chinese soldiers raised the Chinese flag in Hong Kong. The people of Hong Kong felt simultaneously proud of their Chinese heritage and their unique Hong Kong identity. The people of Hong Kong hoped that in the years and decades to come, China would increasingly come to resemble it’s most radiant and dynamic city. The rest of the world was electrified by a sense of optimism that Hong Kong was a glimpse into China’s future, not that Hong Kong would grow into a reflection of China’s past.
